---
title: "Quarto Basics"
format: live-html
jupyter: python3
pyodide:
  packages:
    - matplotlib
    - numpy
    - scipy
    - pandas
---

# My demo

Some markdown text here. More text to come.

Some formulas here.

$$\int f(x) dx = I_\alpha$$

::: {.column-margin}
We know from *the first fundamental theorem of calculus* that for $x$ in $[a, b]$:

$$\frac{d}{dx}\left( \int_{a}^{x} f(u)\,du\right)=f(x).$$
:::

Some interactive python cells here.

```{pyodide}
#| autorun: true
import numpy as np
x = np.arange(3)
print(x)
print(x**2)
```

```{pyodide}
#| autorun: true
y = x + 2
print(y)
```
